methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Zap Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Radiant, a Series D nuclear energy company founded by Doug Bernauer, has raised $465M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Zap Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow nuclear energy set. Only one Series D peer was identified, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical credibility. Stage-progression analysis was therefore the primary triangulation method. This approach applied a 1.5x step-up multiplier—typical for Series D advancement—to Radiant's last post-money valuation of $1.0B, yielding an implied value of $1.5B.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.5B. The $400M range reflects uncertainty inherent in relying on one quantitative method; absent additional narrow-set comparable data or transaction-based reference points, the median estimate should be treated as directional. Key uncertainties include technology validation maturity relative to peers and the timing of near-term financing or exit events that might anchor valuation more precisely within this band.
